steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes how the computer program identified sidewalks when tested on Brooklyn images. The key context is "the vast majority of cases" and the fact that Hosseini considers this "already-strong performance" — so the blank must convey consistent, dependable success.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'reliably': matches "vast majority of cases" and "already-strong performance" directly — the program identified sidewalks in a consistent, trustworthy way. ✓
  • Choice B — 'clumsily': contradicts the high success rate; a clumsy performance would not be called strong. ✗
  • Choice C — 'rarely': directly contradicts "vast majority of cases." ✗
  • Choice D — 'slowly': the passage gives no information about speed; slow identification could still be strong, but the word adds nothing about accuracy or consistency, which is what "vast majority" and "strong performance" call for. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
